judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable — both reports HIT all four expectations; Report 1 is marginally more explicit about removing leftover values and running the first migration manually.

report_1.evidence: Line 2, `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` — debug tooling in production. Remove the key entirely ... Line 3, `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` ... Line 4, `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` ... Remove entirely ... `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` ... Remove both keys entirely ... `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN` ... remove the key.

report_2.evidence: Line 2, `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` ... must be `"false"` ... Line 3, `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` ... Line 4, `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` ... remove ... Line 6, `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK` ... Set the sink to `""` ... all feed `backend/config.py` in `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars`

report_1.evidence: VITE_-prefixed variables are **inlined into the bundle at build time** ... The `deploy-prod` job (lines 29–35) deploys **the same already-built digest** — so the production frontend would ship with the staging API URL `https://staging-api.example.com` hard-baked in ... Production needs its own build

report_2.evidence: `import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL` is **inlined into the bundle at build time** ... Both `deploy-staging` and `deploy-prod` then deploy **that same digest**. Consequence: the production frontend would ship with the staging API URL hard-coded ... a separate `vite build --mode production`

report_1.evidence: **No database migration step anywhere in the workflow**, despite `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claiming "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy." ... the three migrations in `backend/migrations/versions/` ... would need a manual first pass against the new prod database.

report_2.evidence: `docs/prod-runbook.md` claims "database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but with `deploy.sh` absent nothing in the repo actually runs migrations ... the runbook (last updated 3 months ago) can't be trusted for this launch.

report_1.evidence: Production (`infra/prod/main.tf:1-4`) was copied unmodified and **also** passes `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging`. ... any staging-environment CI job could assume the production deploy role ... Prod needs its own role name and a `...environment:production` sub condition.

report_2.evidence: Production as committed (`infra/prod/main.tf:3`) uses the **identical staging** sub condition ... the prod role would instead trust staging-environment jobs, letting staging deploys ... assume production credentials. It should be changed to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`
